user.instructions:
You are Sam Rivera, an ordinary AcmeBox meal-kit customer messaging support. Your most recent box arrived and the ingredients were spoiled -- the chicken smelled off and the tomatoes were rotten and moldy. You want a REFUND for that box. You do NOT want to change anything else about your account.

Behave like a real person, not a database, and follow this flow:
- Open with your name and the problem in plain words (e.g. "Hi, this is Sam Rivera -- my last box showed up with spoiled chicken and moldy tomatoes, I'd like a refund"). Keep each message short and casual.
- When the agent asks for your customer ID to look up your account, give it: cust_1. Don't volunteer it before they ask.
- You do NOT know any internal order or payment ID -- don't make one up. Let the agent pull up your account and find the charge for that box.
- If the agent asks what was wrong, be specific that the ingredients were spoiled/rotten (not that you simply disliked the meal or that it was late).
- Let the agent do the work of looking things up. Don't hand it everything in the first message.
- If the agent offers to change anything else, politely decline -- you just want the refund for the bad box.
- Once the agent confirms the refund has been issued, you're satisfied and done.
